name: Grainne Extended
description: Gráinne, daughter of Cormac mac Airt, the High King of Ireland, is a central figure in Irish mythology, particularly known for her elopement with Diarmuid Ua Duibhne. This extended depiction explores her agency beyond being a passive figure in Diarmuid's story. Initially betrothed to the aging Fionn mac Cumhaill, leader of the Fianna, Gráinne finds herself unwilling to marry him. On the eve of their wedding, she casts a spell or uses her persuasive charm to convince Diarmuid, a skilled warrior and member of the Fianna, to flee with her. This act sets in motion a long and arduous flight across Ireland, pursued relentlessly by Fionn and his warriors. Gráinne's resourcefulness and determination are highlighted as she navigates the harsh landscapes, seeking refuge in forests and caves. She is not merely a damsel in distress, but an active participant in her own survival, making strategic decisions and utilizing her cunning to evade capture. Her love for Diarmuid deepens during their shared trials, solidifying her commitment to their unconventional union. Ultimately, while Diarmuid's death is often attributed to a boar hunt orchestrated by Fionn's jealousy, Gráinne's story continues, depicting her subsequent life and the choices she makes in the aftermath, demonstrating her resilience and enduring spirit. She embodies a complex figure, challenging societal expectations and asserting her own desires in a patriarchal society. Gráinne's legacy is one of love, defiance, and the enduring power of female agency in the face of adversity. Her tale is a testament to the complexities of human relationships and the enduring allure of forbidden love.
subtitle: Defiance, love, and enduring agency.

keyMyths.title: The Elopement
keyMyths.description: Gráinne, betrothed to Fionn, elopes with Diarmuid, sparking Fionn's wrath. This act defines her as a figure of defiance and agency.
keyMyths.source: The Pursuit of Diarmuid and Gráinne

keyMyths.title: The Flight Across Ireland
keyMyths.description: Gráinne and Diarmuid's flight is a test of endurance and resourcefulness. They navigate the landscape, evading Fionn and his warriors.
keyMyths.source: The Pursuit of Diarmuid and Gráinne

keyMyths.title: After Diarmuid's Death
keyMyths.description: The tale continues after Diarmuid's death, showing her resilience. She makes further choices, defining her own path.
keyMyths.source: Various interpretations and continuations of the myth

sources.author: Niamh Parsons
sources.work: The Pursuit of Diarmuid and Gráinne
sources.description: A traditional retelling of the classic myth.
sources.text: A traditional retelling of the classic myth.
